source.title: Mekonnen & Hoekstra — Water Footprint of Crops (Report 47)
source.note: Cucumber — water_per_kg: 240 L/kg used. Report 47 Table A1 reports 353 L/kg. Dataset figure is 32% lower than the peer-reviewed value. The discrepancy may reflect greenhouse production dominance (China, Netherlands) which achieves higher yields per m³. Value is in a plausible range; flagged but not corrected.

Poore & Nemecek (2018) — Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers (Science)
source.note: Sweet potato — emissions_per_kg: 0.59 kg CO2e/kg (P&N 2018 global median; low-input tropical root crop; sweet potato is not disaggregated separately in P&N and falls within the 'Root Vegetables' range of 0.43 kg CO2e/kg; 0.59 reflects slightly higher emissions from tropical smallholder systems with greater N2O variability).

source.title: Our World in Data — GHG Emissions per kg of Food (Poore & Nemecek 2018)
source.note: Sweet potato — emissions_per_kg: 0.43 kg CO2e/kg is the P&N 2018 'Root Vegetables' category median from the OWID dataset; 0.59 used in this record is the upper-end estimate accounting for tropical smallholder system variability; both values are within the published range.

source.title: USDA NASS — Agricultural Chemical Use Survey
FAO (2021) — World Food and Agriculture Statistical Pocketbook
source.note: Garlic — fertilizer_kg_ha: ~180 kg/ha (high-value vegetable; N-intensive to achieve marketable bulb size).

source.title: Poore & Nemecek (2018) — Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers (Science)
source.note: Garlic — emissions_per_kg: 0.63 kg CO2e/kg; garlic is not disaggregated in P&N 2018; value interpolated from the 'Onions & Leeks' median of 0.5 kg CO2e/kg and 'Other Vegetables' median of 0.53 kg CO2e/kg, adjusted upward to 0.63 to reflect garlic's higher fertilizer intensity (~180 kg N/ha) driving additional N2O emissions.

source.title: Our World in Data — GHG Emissions per kg of Food (Poore & Nemecek 2018)
source.note: Garlic — emissions_per_kg: 0.5 kg CO2e/kg is the P&N 2018 'Onions & Leeks' median from the OWID dataset, which is the closest published analogue for garlic (Allium family); 0.63 is used as the record value to reflect garlic's higher fertilizer intensity.

source.title: FAO (2021) — World Food and Agriculture Statistical Pocketbook
source.note: Garlic — fertilizer_kg_ha: ~180 kg/ha (high-value vegetable; N-intensive to achieve marketable bulb size).

source.title: Poore & Nemecek (2018) — Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers (Science)
source.note: Garlic — emissions_per_kg: 0.63 kg CO2-eq/kg (vegetable; similar to onions in P&N 2018).
